name: Winner Determination Problem - CR1
content: Items are now item types with available quantities rather than unique single-copy items. Each bid requests specified quantities of several item types, and a bid may be accepted only if the auction still has enough units available for every requested type. The aim remains to choose accepted bids that maximize total revenue.
value_info: `n_items`: Integer number of item types available in the auction.
`bid_values`: List[int] where `bid_values[b]` is the value of bid `b`.
`bid_items`: List[List[int]] where `bid_items[b]` lists the 1-based item-type ids requested by bid `b`.
`bid_quantities`: List[List[int]] where `bid_quantities[b][k]` is the quantity requested for item type `bid_items[b][k]` in bid `b`.
`item_capacities`: List[int] of length `n_items`, where `item_capacities[i]` is the total available quantity of item type `i+1`.
`ref_opt_val`: Optional integer reference optimum for `revenue`; null until filled in.

var2.descr: `revenue`: Integer total value collected from the accepted bids.

complexity.difficulty_rationale: This CR keeps the same bid-selection structure and objective. It only replaces the single-copy item-capacity constraints with quantity-based capacity constraints over item types.

cr_impact.notes: The CR still uses only the existing bid-selection vector `x`. The four per-item compatibility constraints are rewritten as quantity-capacity constraints using `bid_quantities` and `item_capacities`.
